Flight 007 strayed into Soviet airspace and shot down, mistaken for a spy plane. Accident or deliberate act. Narrated by Robert Powell.
Following the Great War the railways of Great Britain had to regroup, forming four large companies from 1923 known as the 'Big 4'. We follow the progress as each of them developed their own style.
The definitive combat helicopter saw first blood in South East Asia and has been transport, gunship, smoke ship, command centre, MediVac and dozens of other roles. S2 E5
Looted gold was the lifeblood of the Nazis as Hitler used stolen gold to fund the Nazi war machine - without it, World War II might never have happened. S4 E4: Nazi Gold
The closing years of the Second World War; as the D-Day invasion of Normandy brought victory one step closer, people on the Home Front continued to endure rationing and German rocket attacks.
This episode reveals how Hitler manipulated and played games with the Czechs and with the western powers of Britain and France to achieve his ends. Ep1
A definitive film history of British forces in action, from D-Day to the Normandy breakout, featuring newly-discovered film from the Imperial War Museum and the Movietone archive.
Rockets had a huge impact on World War II - hand-held rocket launchers such as the American bazooka and German panzerfaust enabled infantry to tackle tanks head-on. Ep8: Rockets
We look at one of the deadliest storms in United States history which struck the nation's southern coast in August 2005. Hurricane Katrina rampaged through the Gulf Coast communities.
A look at the recruitment and deployment of troops on both sides of the clash, the problems faced by the soldiers, the slow pace of the fighting and the establishment of trench warfare. E3
The mighty Flying Fortress takes to the air as we examine the background and development of one of the most iconic bombers from WWII. Serving with both the USAAF and the RAF. S2 E1

Our countdown of America's biggest mysteries continues with the story of the death of the first man of magic Harry Houdini, one of the old West's most prolific gunslingers, and the great moon hoax. E3
The strike ends with no concessions won by the miners, Jessie's headmaster proposes to her, and Matt tells Jack that Dolly is pregnant and agrees to marry her. S1 E6: Empire Day on the Slag Heap
Arthur enters the food business, but when the chef he employs goes on the run from a group of gangsters, Arthur is left to cater for a policeman's retirement party. S8 E10: Too Many Crooks
Cornwall, 1783: Ross Poldark (Aidan Turner) returns from the battlefield to find his father dead, his estate in ruins, and his sweetheart Elizabeth engaged to his cousin. S1 E1
After an attempt to stop a prison riot goes badly wrong and one of their own is left behind, the team try everything they can to save their colleague. S3 E6

Donald Campbell chased a 300-mph record on Coniston Water in an ageing boat and tight finances. Narrated by Robert Powell.

The 1930s were the years of the depression and had a marked effect on Britain's railways. However the renewal of locomotives was urgent so there was considerable change in the steam locomotive fleets.
The F-15 Eagle immediately turned the tide of the first Gulf War to prove its greatness, and was involved in one of the most bizarre combat incidents during that conflict. S2 E6
An investigation into the systematic looting that took place in Paris during four years of Nazi occupation that tracks missing treasures to Berlin and Switzerland. Ep5: City of Loot
Some of the most shocking cases connected with the Nazi regime's bloody reign beginning with Hitler's niece Geli Raubal, who was found dead in his apartment in 1931. Ep1: Hitler's Niece
By September 1938, Hitler had occupied Austria and was intent on invading Czechoslovakia; the British and the French made it clear, though, that they would go to war against Germany if he did so. Ep2
A moving story about the Lancaster pilots who launched an attack on the Tirpitz; the men on both sides are so heroic and dignified, and we hear the story first-hand from these men.

programmes.show_name: Anzacs: In the Face of War
programmes.show_description: Archive footage of Australia and New Zealand forces during WW1, WW2, and the Vietnam conflict; this programme pays homage to those who so bravely sacrificed themselves.
David Beckham has had a football career that most players would be jealous of. In this 2009 retrospective we look back at his career.
The machine gun did not dominate the battlefield of WWII - unlike WWI - but it remained a highly potent weapon including being incorporated into the wings of fighter planes. Ep9: The Machine Gun
Four days before Christmas in 1988, Pan Am flight 103 fell in pieces from the night sky plummeting to the ground in the small Scottish town of Lockerbie where things would never be the same again.
The war dictated the need for new battle strategies and tactics, new weapons technology, and new medical practices to deal with obstacles and situations never previously experienced in warfare. E4
The B-24 Liberator more than proved its worth over Europe and the Pacific by patterning the B17 to deliver a blow to the Axis powers in WWII; however, it wasn't simply a bomb delivery system. S2 E2
Our look at mysteries that have baffled America continues with a look at the greatest unsolved art theft in history, the unexplained death of America's first mystery writer and if Bigfoot exists. E4
Bella turns the front of the house into a shop using Bill's compensation, Jessie gets engaged to Arthur, and Jack marries Dolly at a registry office. S1 E7: A First Time for Everything
Ray is assigned to look after a casino owner's wife, who has become a compulsive gambler. S8 E11: The Odds Couple
When Miss Jones raises suspicions about new tenant Madeline, Rigsby is not too bothered about her brand of 'modern interpretive dancing' but isn't that keen on her partner. S3 E5: Fawcett's Python
Miss Courtney is delighted when the man she was supposed to marry 25 years ago comes back into her life. S2 E3: Brief Re-Encounter
Having almost finished his report into Gene Hunt and his team, DCI Keats continues to push Alex to find out what really happened to Sam Tyler. S3 E7
